restaurants.name: Le Baratin
restaurants.description: Tiny zinc-bar bistro in Belleville beloved by chefs on days off. Raquel Carena's cooking is exceptional.
restaurants.must_try: Whatever the blackboard says. The wine list is extraordinary.
restaurants.booking_tip: Book weeks ahead. Lunch only on weekdays.

restaurants.name: Du Pain et des Idées
restaurants.description: Paris's most beautiful bakery in a preserved Belle Époque interior. The escargot pastries are life-changing.
restaurants.must_try: Escargot pistache-chocolat, pain des amis loaf
restaurants.booking_tip: Opens 7:45am, closed weekends. Queue starts early — worth it.

restaurants.name: Septime
restaurants.description: Bertrand Grébaut's flagship — seasonal, zero-waste, extraordinary. One of the most exciting restaurants in France.
restaurants.must_try: Set menu only — trust the kitchen
restaurants.booking_tip: Book 2-3 months ahead via their website. Lunch is slightly easier to get.

restaurants.name: Marché d'Aligre
restaurants.description: Paris's most authentically local market. Cheap produce, North African stalls, great cheese vendor. No tourists.
restaurants.must_try: Roquefort from the cheese stall, fresh olives from the Moroccan vendor
restaurants.booking_tip: Tuesday-Sunday mornings, closes around 1pm. Cash preferred.

excursions.name: Catacombs of Paris (off-route)
excursions.description: Six million bones arranged in eerie patterns beneath the city. More atmospheric than any museum.
excursions.booking_tip: Book online at catacombes.paris.fr — sells out weeks ahead. Timed entry only.

excursions.name: Promenade Plantée (Coulée Verte)
excursions.description: The original High Line — an elevated railway converted to a garden walkway 20 years before New York's. Quiet and beautiful.
excursions.booking_tip: Start at Bastille end. Walk east. Descend for coffee in the Viaduc des Arts below.

excursions.name: Marché aux Puces de Saint-Ouen
excursions.description: The world's largest antique market. 3,000 stalls across 7 interconnected markets. Bric-a-brac to Baroque furniture.
excursions.booking_tip: Saturday-Monday only. Metro Line 4 to Porte de Clignancourt. Get there before 11am.

hidden_gems.name: Passage Brady
hidden_gems.description: A covered 19th-century passage now entirely occupied by Indian and Pakistani restaurants and spice shops. Surreal and delicious.

hidden_gems.name: Musée de la Chasse et de la Nature
hidden_gems.description: A museum of hunting and nature in a 17th-century hôtel particulier, curated like a contemporary art exhibition. Completely overlooked.

hidden_gems.name: Canal de l'Ourcq at dawn
hidden_gems.description: Walk the eastern stretch of the canal at sunrise. Joggers, fishermen, graffiti walls, no tourists whatsoever.

markets.name: Marché d'Aligre
markets.description: Most local market in Paris. North African vendors, great cheese and produce.

markets.name: Marché des Enfants Rouges
markets.description: Paris's oldest covered market (1615). Lunch stalls from Morocco, Japan, Lebanon, Italy.

seasonal_notes.label: Winter (Dec-Feb)
seasonal_notes.notes: Cold and grey but atmospheric. Christmas markets at La Défense and Champs-Élysées in December. Fewer tourists, museums uncrowded. Push indoor experiences — covered passages, museums, wine bars.

seasonal_notes.label: Spring (Mar-May)
seasonal_notes.notes: Best season. Parisians flood the parks. Cherry blossom in Parc de Sceaux (30 min by RER). Terrasse culture begins in April. Book restaurants further ahead.

seasonal_notes.label: Summer (Jun-Aug)
seasonal_notes.notes: Paris Plages turns the Seine banks into a beach in July-August. Hot, can exceed 35°C — schedule outdoor slots before noon. Many local restaurants close in August.

seasonal_notes.label: Autumn (Sep-Nov)
seasonal_notes.notes: Second-best season. Harvest, beautiful light, locals return from August holidays. Nuit Blanche all-night art festival in October.
